dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6495 IH: Taxpayer Notification and Privacy Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Steube (for himself and Mr. Panetta) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide for specific taxpayer notice when information is sought from third parties.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Taxpayer Notification and Privacy Act.

subsection.text: Paragraph (1) of section 7602(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking and at the end of subparagraph (A),
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: in any case in which the information sought to be obtained from such other persons has not been previously requested from the taxpayer and could reasonably be provided by the taxpayer, identifies each specific item of information intended to be sought from such persons, and

paragraph.text: by amending subparagraph (C), as redesignated by paragraph (2), to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: except as otherwise provided by the Secretary, provides the taxpayer with reasonable opportunity and a period of not less than 45 days (or more, if the taxpayer requests additional time and shows reasonable cause) to respond, including by providing the information described in subparagraph (B), before contact is made with such other persons.

subsection.text: Section 7602(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) as clauses (i), (ii), and (iii), respectively, and by moving such clauses 2 ems to the right,
by striking Exceptions.—This subsection and inserting “Exceptions.—
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) (and so much of subparagraph (C) of paragraph (1) as relates to such subparagraph (B)) shall not apply to information sought from a person other than the taxpayer if—

clause.text: such information is sought with respect to the collection of a tax liability, or

the Secretary determines that such information is necessary notwithstanding whether the taxpayer could independently provide such information or whether such information has been previously requested from the taxpayer,

subsection.text: The amendments made by this subsection shall apply to notices provided under section 7602(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 after the date that is 12 months after the date of the enactment of this Act.
